content: The lighthouse keeper lived alone on the jagged cliff, his only companion the rhythmic sweep of the beacon. Every night for forty years, he guided nameless ships through the hungry fog. He never saw the faces of those he saved, only the distant, fading lights of hulls passing safely into the dark. One stormy evening, a small bottle washed ashore. Inside was a weathered note: "Thank you for the light." He sat by his hearth, the storm howling against the stone walls. For the first time, the silence of the tower didn't feel lonely; it felt like a quiet, shared promise.
